subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
Gizmos includes:

- **1 energy dispenser** (a 3D marble dispenser tower)
- **52 energy marbles** in 4 colors: red, blue, yellow, black
- **108 gizmo cards** in 3 levels (I, II, III) with varying costs and effects
- **4 player dashboards** with energy ring (storage), archive slot, and gizmo display areas
- **4 summary cards** (player aids)
- **Victory point tokens**

The energy dispenser is the centerpiece — marbles are loaded in and 6 are visible in the tray at the bottom for drafting.

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Assemble the **energy dispenser** and load all 52 marbles into it. They roll down and **6 marbles** are visible in the tray at the bottom.
2. Separate gizmo cards by level (I, II, III) and shuffle each deck.
3. Deal face-up gizmo cards into a **display**:
   - **Level I**: 4 face-up cards
   - **Level II**: 3 face-up cards
   - **Level III**: 2 face-up cards
4. Place the remaining decks face down as draw piles.
5. Set victory point tokens in a supply if needed.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Each player takes a **dashboard** with:
   - An **energy ring** that holds up to **5 marbles** (expandable with gizmos).
   - An **archive area** that holds up to **1 card** (expandable with gizmos).
   - Slots for built gizmos organized by trigger type.
2. Each player starts with a **starter gizmo** (already built) that provides a basic ability.
3. Each player draws **random starting energy** — typically **0 marbles** (all energy must be picked during the game).
4. Determine a starting player randomly. Play proceeds clockwise.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
Gizmos is an **engine-building** game where you collect energy marbles and use them to build gizmo machines. Each gizmo you build adds a new triggered ability to your engine.

The magic of Gizmos is **chain reactions**: when you perform an action, your built gizmos may trigger, which may trigger other gizmos, creating cascading combos. A well-built engine turns a single action into a flurry of free picks, builds, and points.

The game ends when any player builds their **4th Level III gizmo** or their **16th gizmo total**. The player with the most **victory points** wins.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, perform **one action** (then resolve all triggered gizmos):

### Action 1: Pick
Take **1 energy marble** from the 6 visible in the dispenser tray and add it to your energy ring. A new marble rolls down to replace it.

### Action 2: File
Take **1 gizmo card** from the face-up display and place it in your **archive**. (You can build it later without it being available to opponents.) Your archive has a **limited capacity** (starts at 1, expandable).

### Action 3: Build
Build a gizmo card by paying its **energy cost** (discard marbles of the required colors from your energy ring). You can build from:
- The **face-up display**, or
- Your **archive**

Place the built gizmo on your dashboard in the appropriate trigger category. You now have that ability permanently.

### Action 4: Research
Draw cards from the **top of a level deck** (I, II, or III) — draw a number of cards equal to your research limit (starts at 3, expandable). Choose **1 card** to file in your archive or build immediately. Return the rest to the bottom of the deck.

After your action, **resolve all triggered gizmos** before the next player's turn.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Gizmo Types (by trigger)
Each gizmo triggers on a specific condition:

- **Pick triggers** (when you pick a marble): "When you pick a red marble, gain 1 additional marble from the dispenser."
- **File triggers** (when you file a card): "When you file a card, pick 1 marble from the dispenser."
- **Build triggers** (when you build a gizmo): "When you build a Level II gizmo, gain 1 VP."
- **Converter triggers** (activate during your turn): "Convert 1 yellow marble into any 2 marbles of your choice."
- **Upgrade triggers** (passive): "Your energy ring holds 2 additional marbles" or "Your archive holds 1 additional card."

### Chain Reactions
This is the heart of the game:
1. You **Pick** a red marble → a pick-trigger gizmo fires, giving you a free blue marble.
2. Gaining the blue marble → another pick-trigger fires, giving you a free yellow marble.
3. And so on.

A single Pick action can cascade into picking 3–4 marbles. A single Build action can trigger free picks, VP bonuses, and more builds.

### Energy Costs
- **Level I gizmos**: Cost 1 marble (any color or a specific color).
- **Level II gizmos**: Cost 2–3 marbles.
- **Level III gizmos**: Cost 4–5 marbles.

Higher-level gizmos have more powerful effects and more VP.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Energy Ring Limit
You can hold a maximum number of marbles in your energy ring (starts at **5**). Upgrade gizmos increase this. If you would gain a marble but your ring is full, the marble goes back to the dispenser.

### Archive Limit
You can hold a limited number of cards in your archive (starts at **1**). Upgrade gizmos increase this. If your archive is full, you cannot file new cards.

### Research Limit
When you research, you draw a number of cards equal to your research limit (starts at **3**). Upgrade gizmos increase this.

### Marble Dispenser
The dispenser always has **6 visible marbles** in the tray. When one is taken, a new one rolls down randomly. If the dispenser runs out, return all spent marbles to it.

### Build Limit per Turn
There is **no limit** to how many gizmos can trigger in a chain. However, each specific gizmo can only trigger **once per turn** (no infinite loops).

### Display Refill
When a card is taken from the face-up display, immediately replace it from the corresponding level's draw pile.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
### Game End Trigger
The game ends when any player achieves either:
- Built their **4th Level III gizmo**, OR
- Built their **16th gizmo total** (across all levels)

Finish the current round so all players have had the same number of turns.

### Scoring
Each gizmo card has a **VP value** printed on it:
- **Starter gizmo**: 0 VP
- **Level I**: 0–1 VP
- **Level II**: 1–3 VP
- **Level III**: 3–7 VP

Additionally, some gizmos grant **bonus VP tokens** during the game (from build triggers, etc.).

### Final Calculation
1. Sum VP from all **built gizmo cards**.
2. Add any **bonus VP tokens** earned.
3. Cards in your archive or unbuilt are worth **0 VP**.

### Winning
The player with the **highest VP total** wins. Tiebreaker: most total gizmos built.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Build early, build often**: Every gizmo makes your engine stronger. Level I gizmos are cheap and start your combos.
- **Pick triggers are king**: Gizmos that trigger when you pick marbles create the longest chains, since picking leads to more picking.
- **Expand your energy ring**: Running out of marble storage limits your combos. Upgrade capacity early.
- **Don't ignore Level I cards**: They're cheap and many have powerful trigger effects that form the backbone of combos.
- **Watch the colors**: Focus your engine on 1–2 marble colors. Gizmos that trigger on specific colors are stronger when you draft those colors consistently.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Combo engine**: Focus on pick-triggers that chain into each other. Build 3–4 pick-triggers that feed each other, then every Pick action floods your ring with marbles.
- **Color specialization**: Concentrate on gizmos and energy of one color. A red-focused engine with "when you pick red" triggers becomes incredibly efficient.
- **Converter engine**: Build converter gizmos that transform cheap marbles into expensive ones. This lets you build Level III gizmos cheaply.
- **Rush strategy**: Race to 4 Level III gizmos by building an efficient marble-collection engine and focusing on expensive builds.
- **Wide strategy**: Build many Level I–II gizmos quickly, aiming for the 16-gizmo trigger. Each small gizmo contributes VP and trigger effects.
- **File-and-build**: Use file triggers to gain resources, then build from archive to control which cards you access without opponents sniping them.
- **Deny key cards**: If you see a gizmo that would supercharge an opponent's engine, file it or build it yourself — even if it's not ideal for you.
